notes: Skeleton reads this to decide which wizard stages to run for a given goal. The 'difficulty' field on a catalog entry (or the tunnel runWizard payload) selects a tier. When not provided, auto_classify.defaults_to wins. Tuning this file is the LEARN-loop seam for performance vs. quality.

L1.rationale: Simple CRUD / single-view / static content. One LLM call, no planner, no CLI agentic session, no autofix. Evaluator still runs to feed the rubric ledger, but its result does not trigger a fix turn. Typical wall-clock 30-90s. SL.5: estimate baseline = $0.05/run (gpt-4o-mini single turn ~3k in + ~3k out).

L2.rationale: Default tier. Planner scopes the spec, generator uses CLI-first with API fallback, evaluator critiques and triggers one autofix cycle. SL.5: estimate baseline = $0.15/run (planner + generator + evaluator + one fix cycle).

L3.rationale: Framework-heavy or stateful goals. Deep planning runs ahead of the simple planner, generator gets extended budget, multiple autofix cycles allowed. SL.5: estimate baseline = $0.40/run (deep plan + generator 32k + evaluator + 2 fix cycles, higher variance).

auto_classify.notes: Used ONLY when the caller does not pass an explicit difficulty. Catalog-based flywheel runs always pass difficulty explicitly and bypass this.
